description: REAL wire capture (tests/stacks_client_check harness input) for the wave-6B part 4 Study/Converse client, captured from the actual engine via FastAPI TestClient driving join -> create_hero -> breach into the seed-6 gothic_living_study room (docs/INFINITE_STACKS_CONTRACTS.md S5.11), exactly the way tests/fixtures/stacks_ui/puzzle_mystery_chamber.json was captured for wave 3. `heroId` is the acting hero's own id; `snapshotMessage` is the {kind:'snapshot', view, revision} shape reduceServerMessage consumes directly. `beforeInteractSnapshotMessage` is the SAME room before any object interaction (baseline object list, empty promoted ledgers). `socialCheckResolvedEvent` is one real social_check_resolved event payload verbatim (from a converse call appealing to Elara's disclosed objective_protect_the_letters), for the ceremony-renders-the-event-payload-verbatim test. `otherHeroId` is a second hero who joined but never entered the study room, for the appeal-picker/objects privacy test (their own snapshot never carries this room's promoted ledger contents).

abilities.name: Keen Observer
abilities.fallback: You don't have to think about reading a room anymore. You just do it.
abilities.accessible: Ability: Keen Observer. Trigger: the instant you breach into a new room, before you spend a card, action, or Energy on anything else. Effect: you get a free Insight + Read check against DC 11. Why it's good: a clean success on that check hands you one concrete, actionable detail about the room -- a hidden exit, an odd object, a discrepancy worth acting on -- every single room, for free, all game.

abilities.name: Plain Speaking
abilities.fallback: One short, unambiguous sentence, aimed so everyone in earshot understands it the same way.
abilities.accessible: Ability: Plain Speaking. Trigger: manually invoked, once per room, no card or action spent. Effect: you get a free Presence + Wordcraft check against DC 8. Why it's good: it's a low-DC check on demand -- when a room's social read is close, this is a guaranteed extra roll to make sure your warning actually lands, at zero resource cost.

abilities.name: Keen Observer
abilities.fallback: You don't have to think about reading a room anymore. You just do it.
abilities.accessible: Ability: Keen Observer. Trigger: the instant you breach into a new room, before you spend a card, action, or Energy on anything else. Effect: you get a free Insight + Read check against DC 11. Why it's good: a clean success on that check hands you one concrete, actionable detail about the room -- a hidden exit, an odd object, a discrepancy worth acting on -- every single room, for free, all game.

abilities.name: Plain Speaking
abilities.fallback: One short, unambiguous sentence, aimed so everyone in earshot understands it the same way.
abilities.accessible: Ability: Plain Speaking. Trigger: manually invoked, once per room, no card or action spent. Effect: you get a free Presence + Wordcraft check against DC 8. Why it's good: it's a low-DC check on demand -- when a room's social read is close, this is a guaranteed extra roll to make sure your warning actually lands, at zero resource cost.

objects.name: Faded Reading Rug
objects.fallback: A heavy, faded rug lies flat before the hearth, one corner curled up as if kicked aside in a hurry.
objects.accessible: Object: reading rug. State: undisturbed, but one corner is visibly curled -- an inconsistency worth investigating.

interactions.fallback: The curled corner doesn't match the rest of the rug's wear pattern -- something moved it recently.
interactions.accessible: Looking at the rug in either state reveals the curled-corner inconsistency.

interactions.fallback: You pull the rug fully back. Beneath it, a rectangle of floorboard has been cut and re-fitted -- a hidden panel.
interactions.accessible: Moving the rug while undisturbed transitions it to displaced and reveals the hidden floor compartment.

objects.name: Cold Hearth
objects.fallback: The hearth has been cold for a long time; a few unburned logs remain stacked beside it.
objects.accessible: Object: fireplace. State: cold, unlit, with unburned logs stacked beside it.

interactions.fallback: Raking through the cold ash, you find a half-burned scrap of paper -- someone tried to destroy something here.
interactions.accessible: Searching the fireplace in either state reveals a half-burned scrap of paper.

interactions.fallback: You strike a light and the logs catch, filling the study with warmth.
interactions.accessible: Lighting the fireplace while cold transitions it to lit.

interactions.fallback: You smother the flames; the study returns to its cold hush.
interactions.accessible: Extinguishing the fireplace while lit transitions it back to cold.

interactions.fallback: Standing before the lit hearth, the warm draft settles the dust in the air and clears your stinging eyes.
interactions.accessible: Using the lit fireplace to warm up clears the Confused condition from the dust hazard.

objects.name: Writing Desk
objects.fallback: A heavy writing desk faces the door, its drawers shut, an inkwell dry beside a stack of blank paper.
objects.accessible: Object: writing desk. State: closed drawers.

interactions.fallback: Sorted correspondence, all addressed to 'E.V.' -- someone who signed every letter the same careful way.
interactions.accessible: Searching the desk while open reveals correspondence addressed to E.V.

interactions.fallback: The top drawer sticks, then gives way all at once.
interactions.accessible: Opening the desk while closed transitions it to open.

objects.name: Reading Chairs
objects.fallback: Two high-backed chairs sit angled toward the hearth, as if mid-conversation.
objects.accessible: Object: reading chairs. State: upright, angled toward the hearth.

interactions.fallback: Settling into the near chair, you notice its cushion has been recently flattened -- someone sat here often, and recently.
interactions.accessible: Sitting in the chairs reveals recent, frequent use.

interactions.fallback: A second cushion, untouched, still bears the shape of someone who stopped visiting.
interactions.accessible: Inspecting the chairs reveals one cushion is undisturbed, hinting at an absent second occupant.

objects.name: Portrait of a Young Woman
objects.fallback: A small portrait hangs crookedly beside the bookshelf: a young woman, pen in hand, smiling at whoever painted her.
objects.accessible: Object: portrait. State: hung, slightly crooked.

interactions.fallback: The plaque beneath reads: 'Elara Vance, Keeper of the Third Stack.'
interactions.accessible: Looking at the portrait reveals the plaque naming Elara Vance, Keeper of the Third Stack.

interactions.fallback: Straightening the frame, you find nothing behind it but a clean square of unfaded wallpaper -- it has hung here a very long time.
interactions.accessible: Moving the portrait reveals nothing hidden behind it, only a long-undisturbed wall.

objects.name: Bookshelf
objects.fallback: A floor-to-ceiling bookshelf, mostly full, with one gap where a volume was pulled and never returned.
objects.accessible: Object: bookshelf. State: intact, with one visible gap.

interactions.fallback: Behind the gap, wedged flat against the shelf's back panel, is a slim personal ledger.
interactions.accessible: Searching the bookshelf reveals a slim personal ledger hidden behind the gap.

interactions.fallback: You pull down the Field Manual and read a chapter at random.
interactions.accessible: Reading the shelved Field Manual (see book content).

interactions.fallback: Pulling every shelf away from the wall costs precious time, but behind the last one is a narrow service passage leading north out of the study.
interactions.accessible: A thorough search of the bookshelf spends extra Exploration Energy and reveals a service passage to the north.

objects.name: Field Manual: Binding Loose Pages
objects.fallback: A well-worn Field Manual on page-binding technique, spine cracked from heavy use.

interactions.fallback: The manual explains, in dry technical prose, how a loose Knowledge Fragment is bound into an existing book.
interactions.accessible: Reading the Field Manual reveals its structured facts about binding loose pages.

objectives.fallback: Keep her sister's letters from being taken by anyone she doesn't trust.
objectives.accessible: Main objective: protect the hidden letters.

objectives.fallback: Keep the study in order, as if inspection could happen any day.
objectives.accessible: Main objective: maintain the study's order.

objectives.fallback: Be recognized and addressed as Keeper, not dismissed as a ghost or a remnant.
objectives.accessible: Main objective: be addressed by her title.

objects.name: Faded Reading Rug
objects.fallback: The rug is thrown back against the hearth, exposing a rectangle of floorboards cut cleanly from the rest.
objects.accessible: Object: reading rug. State: displaced, revealing a cut floorboard panel beneath.

interactions.fallback: The curled corner doesn't match the rest of the rug's wear pattern -- something moved it recently.
interactions.accessible: Looking at the rug in either state reveals the curled-corner inconsistency.

interactions.fallback: You pull the rug fully back. Beneath it, a rectangle of floorboard has been cut and re-fitted -- a hidden panel.
interactions.accessible: Moving the rug while undisturbed transitions it to displaced and reveals the hidden floor compartment.

objects.name: Floor Compartment
objects.fallback: The panel lifts on a hidden hinge, revealing a shallow strongbox built into the joists.
objects.accessible: Object: floor compartment. State: open, contents visible.

interactions.fallback: Brass fittings, recently oiled -- this compartment has been opened more than once.
interactions.accessible: Inspecting the compartment in either non-hidden state reveals it has been used recently.

interactions.fallback: You lift the panel by its brass ring. Inside: a bundle of letters tied with cord, and a small locked diary.
interactions.accessible: Opening the compartment while closed transitions it to open and reveals its contents.

objects.name: Locked Diary
objects.fallback: The diary lies open, its final entries legible.

interactions.fallback: The clasp gives way easily -- it was never truly locked, only stuck with age. The final entry is dated the night the Spire's corruption reached the Third Stack.
interactions.accessible: Reading the diary (in either state) reveals its structured facts, including an explicitly unreliable final entry.
